Inputs (in priority order):
  - 006-DR-MEMO-mcp-safety.md — three-mode design, refusal scenarios, audit fields, payments-mcp-not-a-directory
  - 007-DR-MEMO-carrier-vocabulary.md — 20-tool catalog with per-tool authenticity verdict, density gaps, Persona 9 underwriting-manager surface
  - 008-DR-MEMO-guidewire-api.md — per-customer-config-dependence spectrum, 9-YAML profile schema (NEW events.yaml), App Events shard-by-primaryObject.id, IG boundary
  - 009-DR-MEMO-harness-runtime.md — TS signatures, hash-chain DDL, idempotency formula, three-mode enforcement
  - 002-DR-CRIT-personas.md — 9 personas (incl. Persona 9 added in PR #28)
  - 07-ROADMAP.md — per-epic exit criteria already authored in PR #30
  - 09-DR-DIAG-architecture.mmd — system diagram (PR #29)

Spawned via subagent because the PRD authoring is large (~3000-5000 words) and benefits from a fresh-context author.

title: [GW-1.10 + GW-1.11] Testing policy + observability spec
description:
Paired PR per Jeremy's 'keep going on 1.10+1.11' directive.

GW-1.10 — tests/TESTING.md
  Per IS Testing SOP (~/000-projects/CLAUDE.md § Intent Solutions Testing SOP):
  - Coverage floor (per-package + repo-wide)
  - Mutation kill rate (Stryker baseline)
  - CRAP threshold
  - Architecture rules (depcruise)
  - Bias count (max false-positive guard tests)
  - Gherkin lint rules for L6 acceptance scenarios
  - All thresholds hash-pinned via audit-harness init
  - 7-layer taxonomy applicability (L1 hooks → L7 acceptance) per the IS standard

GW-1.11 — 05-TECHNICAL-SPEC.md § Observability
  Per 009-DR-MEMO § Observability fan-out + CLAUDE.md Hard Rule #6:
  - Span tree structure on every MCP tool call
  - Required attributes (trace_id, tenant_id, tool_name, mode, actor_id)
  - Pino log shape (JSON in prod, pino-pretty in dev)
  - AppError typed class for Sentry tagging
  - packages/observability/getObservability() factory contract
  - Architecture rules in CI (depcruise + AST checks for span coverage)
  - Sentry → bead auto-creation pipeline (claude_ai_Sentry MCP + bd-sync)
  - Per-environment endpoint configuration (dev: local OTLP collector / Jaeger; prod: user-configured)

Branch: docs/gw-1.10-1.11-testing-observability
PR title: docs(GW-1.10+1.11): testing policy + observability spec
Refs #2.
close_reason: GW-1.10 (tests/TESTING.md) + GW-1.11 (05-TECHNICAL-SPEC.md § 4 Observability + § 6 Quality gates) merged. Hash-pin target ready; AppError + getObservability() factory contracts spec'd; SOC 2 overlay applied; vocab linter rules baked in.

- mcp-safety-reviewer — per-tool blast radius, three-mode design, refusal scenarios, harness gating

title: [Phase 0] Provision Guidewire Cloud sandbox tenant
description:
Hard prereq for the entire project. Without sandbox access, neither the staffed audit panel nor E1 can begin (NO MOCKS rule).

## What we need

- Guidewire Cloud sandbox tenant credentials (OAuth client ID + secret)
- Tenant ID + accessible LOB / typelist / custom-entity surface documented
- SOPS-encrypted creds in `runbook/secrets.prod.sops.yaml`
- Initial replay recordings captured to `tests/recordings/` with provenance

## Reality check (per Jeremy 2026-05-04)

There is **no public Stripe/Twilio-style self-serve sandbox signup**. Guidewire Cloud customers receive dev environments / "**planets**" through their Guidewire **provisioning team** — not via a public form. Non-customers must go through the **PartnerConnect** program or the **Marketplace partner** route to get ecosystem-validated sandbox access.

## Application paths (in priority order)

**1. Guidewire Developers portal** — primary entry point.
   - https://www.guidewire.com/developers
   - Cloud APIs: https://www.guidewire.com/developers/apis/cloud-apis

**2. PartnerConnect Technology Partners** — most likely route to ecosystem-validated sandbox tenant for non-customer Intent Solutions IO.
   - https://www.guidewire.com/partnerconnect/solution/
   - Application: https://www.guidewire.com/partners/for-guidewire-partners-partnerconnect
   - Frame: Intent Solutions IO (LLC) registers as a Technology Partner, citing the planned MCP integration for the Guidewire InsuranceSuite. The OSS repo at github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude is the credibility artifact.

**3. Guidewire Marketplace** — official ecosystem listing path.
   - https://marketplace.guidewire.com/
   - Aligns with the project E11+ goal (publish to claude-code-plugins-plus-skills marketplace plus listing in Guidewire Marketplace as a Claude/Anthropic-native MCP).

**4. Direct Contact / Provisioning Team** (only for existing Cloud customers — N/A for us pre-partnership).
   - https://www.guidewire.com/about/get-in-touch/contact-us
   - Existing Guidewire Cloud customers request a "planet" sandbox via this path. Not the route for an outsider building partner integration.

**5. Public API references** — use IN PARALLEL while applying. No signup required.
   - PolicyCenter Cloud API: https://docs.guidewire.com/cloud/pc/202511/cloudapica/
   - ClaimCenter Cloud API: https://docs.guidewire.com/cloud/cc/202503/apiref/
   - InsuranceSuite Cloud API overview: https://docs.guidewire.com/education/cloud-integration-basics/latest/docs/integration_cloud_basics/system_api_overview/
   - Developer Community: https://community.guidewire.com/s/

The public API docs let us draft tool/client code shapes without sandbox creds. Real recordings + contract tests come AFTER sandbox lands.

## Pivot path (if direct sandbox unobtainable in reasonable time)

Per master plan v4: scope pivots to a vendor-partner integration where API access exists — never to mocks. Candidates: a downstream vendor (One Inc, Smart Communications, ClaimMetrics, CCC Intelligent Solutions) who already has Guidewire-integrated sandbox access and would benefit from MCP tooling against their slice. Vendor partnership replaces direct-Guidewire-sandbox as the dev surface.

## Status check (this bead)

- Owner: Jeremy
- Target: paid sandbox tenant credentials in SOPS, with LOB/typelist/custom-entity documentation
- Updates: post in this issue or via `bd-sync note guidewire-adj "..."`

Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/05-TECHNICAL-SPEC.md (NO MOCKS section)
notes: GitHub: jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude#1 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/issues/1. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note guidewire-adj` and `bd-sync close guidewire-adj` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

title: [GH Pages] Live architecture diagram at demo.intentsolutions.io (custom domain)
description:
Per Jeremy 2026-05-04 'make it the github pages'. Generate a static HTML wrapper that renders 09-DR-DIAG-architecture.mmd client-side via Mermaid.js (no Chrome needed; works around the local mmdc/Puppeteer failure), deploy via GitHub Pages.

Files:
- pages/index.html — static page, Mermaid.js from CDN, dark theme, sandbox-blind copy that contains the .mmd content inlined at deploy time
- .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml — push-to-main trigger (when pages/ or .mmd changes); inlines the .mmd source via sed substitution; uses actions/configure-pages + actions/upload-pages-artifact + actions/deploy-pages

Operator step (post-merge): enable Pages in repo settings via 'gh repo edit --enable-pages' OR Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.

URL on success: https://jeremylongshore.github.io/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/

title: [Workflow phase guards] Gate CI on package.json + remove auto-cut release
description:
Per Jeremy 2026-05-04. Paperwork-phase repo currently has no package.json — default CI from /repo-dress fails noisily. release.yml auto-cuts on push to main which produced an undesired v0.0.1 bump. Goals:

1. Gate ci.yml jobs on hashFiles('package.json') != '' so they no-op until E1 introduces the workspace.
2. Remove the push: branches: [main] trigger from release.yml; keep workflow_dispatch only. Releases become manual until we're past Phase 0 + E1.
3. Update sandbox bead/issue with Jeremy's added nuance: Guidewire Cloud customers get 'planets' (dev environments) via provisioning team only — non-customers must go through PartnerConnect or Marketplace.

Branch: chore/disable-workflows-paperwork-phase
PR title: chore: gate CI + disable auto-release for paperwork phase
Refs #2.
close_reason: Workflow guards merged in PR #21. CI now no-ops until package.json exists; release is workflow_dispatch only; sandbox bead/issue updated with planet-provisioning nuance.

title: [E2] PolicyCenter MCP (read-only) — 5-7 carrier-vocabulary tools
description: MVP epic 2. ONE MCP server: servers/policycenter-mcp/. Read-only tools using carrier-vocabulary names (NOT API verbs): find-submissions-waiting-on-me, whats-our-appetite-on-this-risk, show-policies-for-this-insured, summarize-this-submission, did-we-lose-this-account, explain-why-this-got-referred. Drives against real Guidewire Cloud sandbox via tests/recordings/ replay. Drafting tools (draft-referral-note) ship in E5. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E2 + 02-PRD.md § Epic 2.
notes: GitHub: jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude#4 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/issues/4. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note guidewire-0qf` and `bd-sync close guidewire-0qf` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

title: [E1] Foundation — mcp-runtime, schemas, auth, audit, client-sdk, observability
description: MVP epic 1. Foundational packages: mcp-runtime (transport + SDK wrap), schemas (Zod), auth (Guidewire Hub OAuth + JWT propagation), audit (hash-chain Postgres), client-sdk (renamed from adapter-sdk to drop adapter baggage), observability (OTel + pino + Sentry factory in packages/observability). Includes sub-beads GW-2.1 through GW-2.5: audit-tests classify, install audit-harness, CI/CD GH Actions, hash-pin policies, coverage/mutation baselines. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E1 + 02-PRD.md § Epic 1 + 05-TECHNICAL-SPEC.md (stack).
notes: GitHub: jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude#3 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/issues/3. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note guidewire-4rd` and `bd-sync close guidewire-4rd` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

title: [E3] Harness library + CLI — plan / approve / execute / audit / rollback
description: MVP epic 3. packages/harness/ as library + CLI (NOT an MCP server — recursive). Implements: plan generation, policy gate, human approval flow, execute, hash-chained audit trail, rollback hint, evidence bundle export (JSON). Three execution modes: read_only, draft_only, approved_execute. Hard rule: no audit = no write, no policy decision = no write, no idempotency key = no write. Published as @intentsolutions/guidewire-harness on npm. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E3 + 03-ARCHITECTURE.md (harness layer).
notes: GitHub: jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude#5 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/issues/5. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note guidewire-jpu` and `bd-sync close guidewire-jpu` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

title: [E11+] Publish to claude-code-plugins-plus-skills marketplace
description:
Future epic — package this repo as a Claude Code plugin and publish to the claude-code-plugins-plus-skills marketplace.

Scope:
- Plugin manifest (per IS marketplace standard at ~/000-projects/claude-code-plugins/000-docs/6767-b-SPEC-DR-STND-claude-skills-standard.md)
- SKILL.md frontmatter (8-field IS-enterprise-required set: name, description, allowed-tools, version, author, license, compatibility, tags) for any skills shipped
- Validator pass (~/000-projects/claude-code-plugins/scripts/validate-skills-schema.py at marketplace tier)
- /skill-creator for any new skills (never hand-roll)
- Marketplace README + plugin description copy
- License pinning (Apache-2.0 per E1 plan)

Dependencies: blocked by E1 (foundation), E3 (harness), E4 (cowork starter) at minimum. Realistically lands after E5 or later, when there's a meaningfully complete plugin to publish.

Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E11+ (lands as part of GW-1.6).
notes: GitHub: jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude#14 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/guidewire-mcp-for-claude/issues/14. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note guidewire-qqx` and `bd-sync close guidewire-qqx` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

title: [E10] Onboarding + certification CLI — pnpm gw onboard <customer>
description: Buildout epic 10. Customer onboarding CLI: scaffolds profile mappings only (auth, roles, LOB, typelists, custom entities, field aliases, approval matrix, PII policy), runs contract tests against customer sandbox, produces security-review checklist for handoff. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E10.

description: Buildout epic 9. servers/producer-mcp/ for MGA/broker audience: show-my-book-of-business, whats-my-commission-status, find-my-pending-quotes. Answers persona #4's complaint (producer side underrepresented). Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E9.

title: [E8] BillingCenter MCP + Payments (separate payments-mcp with dual control)
description: Buildout epic 8. servers/billingcenter-mcp/ read-only first: show-overdue-accounts, whats-the-payment-status, find-billing-issues-for-this-policy, reconcile-this-payment. Payments tools split into separate payments-mcp (dual-control review required, NEVER in OSS demo path). Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E8.

title: [E7] ClaimCenter MCP
description: Buildout epic 7. servers/claimcenter-mcp/ with claims-vocabulary tools: find-claims-at-risk-of-leakage, summarize-this-loss, whats-the-reserve-picture, draft-denial-letter. Three-mode design per tool. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E7.

title: [E6] Workflow + Events — webhook receiver, queue, events-mcp (query-only)
description: Buildout epic 6. Event-receiver infra: webhook + BullMQ on Redis (dev) / Cloud Tasks or SQS (prod). Small events-mcp server for query-only / replay access. Integration Gateway triggers move into the relevant suite MCP. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E6 + 03-ARCHITECTURE.md (events plane).

title: [E5] Drafting tools (referral note, endorsement) — first writes
description: Buildout epic 5. Add drafting tools to policycenter-mcp: draft-referral-note, propose-endorsement. Drafts go to harness in draft_only mode — never auto-execute. Human reviews + applies via existing UI. Blueprint: 000-docs/blueprint/07-ROADMAP.md § E5.
